comparator: llama.cpp acd79d603 (Windows CPU-only build) /completion, greedy (temp 0/top_k 1/seed 0), -ngl 0 -ctk f32 -ctv f32 -fa off --no-repack -c 8704
camelid_backend: q8_0 runtime, RTX 3060 Laptop 6 GiB: ALL five buckets fully GPU-resident — the 1.7B weights (1845 MiB) leave a large VRAM-sized resident KV cap (25266 positions) covering every bucket's last KV position (max 8303). No CPU-fallback.
execution_note: Single-model runs only: the llama.cpp oracle was captured alone and its server killed before camelid was started (engines never co-resident), to stay within host RAM.

buckets.note: NOT identical — benign greedy near-tie divergence (see near_tie); not claimed

near_tie.description: At generation index 16 the oracle emits 'CMLD' (→ '...fact 397 is CMLD-8192.') while camelid emits ' the' (→ '...fact 397 is the answer. The answer is CMLD-8192.'). BOTH engines produce the correct hidden code CMLD-8192; camelid simply phrases the answer one token longer. A benign greedy near-tie at the longest (8253-token) context on the smaller 1.7B model (the 4B-Q8_0 sibling is token-identical at this bucket). Not a camelid decode error, but not token-identical to the oracle, so the 8192 bucket is NOT promoted.

claim_boundary: PROMOTED: bounded 512/1024/2048/4096-context (446/968/2013/4088 Qwen3 tokens) raw-decode greedy parity for the exact Qwen3-1.7B-Q8_0 row ONLY — camelid fully-GPU-resident CUDA decode is token-AND-text-identical to the pinned llama.cpp acd79d603 oracle at 50 generated tokens on all four buckets (a contiguous strict-identity prefix). NOT PROMOTED: the 8192 bucket (8253 tokens) diverges at a documented benign greedy near-tie where both engines reach the correct code CMLD-8192 but camelid phrases it one token longer (see near_tie). NOT claimed: the 8192 bucket, model-native/larger context beyond 4096, other Qwen3 sizes/quants, thinking-mode, or throughput.
